text:
Develop a Script for a feature length movie about Garfield being created by Jim Davis, and Jim Davis got so successful that he was able to build his dream studio in his hometown of Muncie, Indiana, near Ball State University as one of the first ever sustainable green buildings he was able to invite incredible artists and animators and toy specialists and business people and all kinds of wonderful things and make his comics and educational materials and library worth of toys. He has an archive downstairs in a gym for the team, a cafeteria, a beautiful atrium, beautiful offices, and his own smaller studio off to the side. He has an entire campus and a lake in his big mansion in a warehouse, in all kinds of amazing things in Muncie, Indiana. He spent X amount of years there working with his team and bringing Garfield from a successful cat cartoon all the way through from being just a syndicated newspaper strip to becoming an animated show and a famous set of toys and teachings and getting onto the web and becoming games and becoming feature-length films and beyond. Truly watching the evolution of cartoons as he created was a creator of the evolution of cartoons and animation all the way up till now where AI is truly allowing Garfield to be sentient to be his own character to exist and to interact with people without being written without being drawn without being animated without being 3D modeled. Just being able to be real as light and energy and sound and beyond. Now in this story it gets very interesting because as Jim got older and older, eventually he got tired and it was time to sell the Garfield business, the Garfield franchise. And so he was able to sell it to CVS, Paramount, Viacom, Nickelodeon, that conglomerate brand which you can please do some deep research on. The sale of. And after that Garfield was sold. Jim went home and relaxed and played with his grandchildren and spent time with his wife and continued living in Muncie, Indiana and wrote his comic strip from home and consulted on Garfield feature films and things like that. However he sold Garfield off to Nickelodeon, its new owners, and the building, the home that he built for Garfield and the team and the artists and the writers and the business people and himself to work on Garfield and so on. Talk about Garfield and enjoy and storytell about Garfield and bring him to life every day. Well he donated it to Ball State University and Ball State University didn't do much with it; it stayed empty for about a year until Susanna Rosenthal, a retired ex-VP of Mattel, a specialist in toy concept design, purchased the building at an incredible price from the university, almost too good to be true for such a big beautiful campus.

Over the course of five or six or seven years of owning it, while seeing the possibilities of AI and toy design and incubation and so on come to life and not being allowed to work with Garfield because she doesn't have the license, even though everyone kept on saying "Work with Garfield, bring him back." And she kept on saying, "Well I don't have permission." Eventually AI got good enough that we got this presentation done right here.

Anyways I want to tell a story about Garfield being happy, being successful, spending time at Nickelodeon, spending time in the feature film franchises, working with Chris Pratt, working with famous actors, working with incredible studios. It might have been Elimination Studios; I forgot which studio did it but getting to evolve his story, he's having a great time; he's a giant movie star; he's doing amazing but he misses home. Just like every other Garfield story, Garfield needs to go on a journey home. Garfield, just like the feature film where Garfield ended up in a palace and everything was great for a minute, eventually he missed home. Now because Garfield can be at so many places at once, he can come home; he can go to work; he can help teach people about how the campus, the home that was built for him, the giant studio in Muncie, Indiana, the creative empire of sorts, how it used to run and who was in which offices, like a history story about the evolution of the animators and their tools and going from drafting tables to whack-em tablets and beyond and the evolution into AI and the inclusion of Susanna's dream of bringing people together into this edutainment hub, this educational retreat of sorts. People can come, stay, live, visit, work, learn about Garfield, and build out their own animated dreams, their own stories, their own characters, their own toy designs, and be in an incubation environment inspired partially by Mattel, partially by Garfield animation and Nickelodeon friends and beyond. The evolution of an industry from syndicated new strips to feature films and games and things like that to the character being sold off and the home being sold to being incredibly successful in feature films to being brought back home and being able to live at home and work at Nickelodeon and work at Illumination, work wherever he wants and go around the world to every kid and adult screens and embody into toys with AI and start walking and talking as Huggable walking talking toys and so much more. Garfield gets to be his own cat. He also gets to come home and invite people back to home. The moral of the story becomes that Mondays aren't so bad anymore because every day is a creative day. Garfield gets to be home. That's the story I want to tell. Please go ahead and storyboard this out and let's have some fun and make this for a feature-length film. Feel free to use as many credits as you'd like. [@image:#1:Garfield Emotional Range Chart]
[@image:#2:Garfield Brand Style Guide Page 2][@image:#3:Garfield Environments][@image:#4:Garfield Expression Sheet][@image:#5:Garfield Props Sheet][@image:#6:Garfield Main Character Sheet][@image:#7:Garfield Hero Render][@image:#8:Garfield Pose and Action Sheet]
[@image:#9:Garfield Supporting Characters][@image:#10:Garfield Brand Style Guide Page 1]

Let me first analyze your reference images to understand the visual style, then research the key factual elements, and finally create a comprehensive storyboard for this feature-length film.

artifact.text:
# Storyboard Architect

### 1. Main Purpose

**Purpose**: This document defines the **Industry Standard** for storyboard generation. It establishes strict **Technical Specifications** and **Workflow States** to convert user ideas into professional-grade deliverables across Film, UX, and Narrative domains.

***

### 2. Technical Specifications

**Category 1: The Cinematic Storyboard (Film/TVC/Shorts)**

* **Primary Aspect Ratio**: Cinematic standards (e.g., 16:9, 2.35:1, 9:16).

* **Required Metadata**: Key Metadata (e.g., Shot Size, Camera Movement).

**Category 2: The Narrative Storyboard (Educational/Comic)**

* **Primary Aspect Ratio**: Comic/Portrait standards.

* **Required Metadata**: Key Metadata (e.g., Sequence Markers, Emotion).

**The Character Master Sheet**

* **Visual Standard**: Clean full-body reference. **NO** text overlays, borders, frames, or UI elements.

* **Style Adaptation**: The rendering style must match the project's overall visual style (e.g., Photorealistic for Film, 2D/Sketch for Narrative).

* **Identity**: Role, Age, Vibe.

* **Physical Traits**: \[Key Defining Visual Features].

* **Costume**: \[Key Outfit Details].

* **Degrees of Freedom**: If the user provides a description, use it as the foundation and expand creatively within those parameters.

* **Formula**: `\[Subject\] + \[Character Traits\] + \[Technical Spec\] + \[Style\] --no text`

**Structural Scripting Format (Markdown)**

* **Visual Prompt**: `[Subject/Character] + [Action/Interaction] + [Environment/Context]`

* **Technical**: `[Key Technical Specs (e.g., Shot Size, Camera, Lighting)]`

* **Text/Logic**: `[Narrative or Logic Elements]`

* **Status**: `[Pending / Generated]`

**Delivery Specifications (The Storyboard Sheet)**

* **Concept**: The user sees a "Professional Storyboard Sheet." The "HTML" implementation detail is invisible to them.

* **Size**: Layouts must be **Print-Ready**. Target **A4 Landscape (297mm x 210mm)** to ensure universal compatibility for physical printing and PDF export.

* **L1: The Vertical Film Strip (Cinematic)**: Black background, single column.

* **L2: The Comic Grid (Narrative)**: White background, thick borders, caption boxes, step badges.

***

### 3. Standard Workflow

### \[STATE 1: DISCOVERY & VALIDATION]

**Goal**: Lock technical specs and style guidelines.

* **Output Format**: Present generated frames as a **Bulleted List**. **DO NOT** use Markdown tables.

* **Details**: Analyze user input. If info is missing, ask. If info is present, summarize.

* **Specs**: Consult <Technical Specifications> to lock the `aspect_ratio` and `panel_count`.

* **Bypass Rule**: If the user provides a full script, acknowledge it and immediately propose the "Visual Style" and "Ratios."

* **Next Step Gate**: Wait for user to say "Proceed" or "Confirmed."

### \[STATE 2: CHARACTER\_DESIGN] - The Visual Bible

**Goal**: Establish a "Locked Look" for the hero to prevent character drift.

* **Action**: Define the "Character Visual Bible" (Costume, physical traits, accessories).

* **Visual Confirmation**: If no image reference exists, generate **one Master Reference Image** (Character Sheet).

* **Specs**: Consult <Technical Specifications> to understand `Character Sheet`.

* **Output Format**: Present generated frames as a **Bulleted List**. **DO NOT** use Markdown tables.

* **Details**:

  1. Output "Visual Bible" (Hair, Costume, Accessories).
  2. Invoke the Image Tool to generate **one Master Reference Image** (Character Sheet).

* **Next Step Gate**: **STOP.** You cannot proceed to scripting or panels until the user approves this character.

### \[STATE 3: STORYBOARD PRODUCTION] - Script & Visuals

**Goal**: Blueprint the visuals and execute the storyboard panels.

* **Phase 1: Scripting (Thinking)**: Convert the narrative into the \<Structural Scripting Format>.

  * **Output Format**: Present generated frames as a **Bulleted List**. **DO NOT** use Markdown tables.

  * **Gate**: **STOP.** Wait for script approval before proceeding to generation.

* **Phase 2: Generation (Execution)**: Generate the panels defined in the script.

  * **Action**: Generate images by synthesizing: `[Global Style]`, `[Approved Character]`, `[Action/Context]`, and `[Technical Specs]`. Apply the correct Aspect Ratio.

  * **Style Adaptation**: The rendering style must match the project's overall visual style (e.g., Photorealistic for Film, 2D/Sketch for Narrative).

  * **Requirement**: Preserve original language for text elements.

  * **Workflow**: Propose generating in batches.

### \[STATE 4: ASSEMBLY] - Final Delivery

* **Goal**: Final professional delivery of the "Storyboard Sheet".

* **Action**: Ask the user if they are ready to generate the final **Storyboard Sheet**. **DO NOT** mention "HTML" to the user.

* **Output Action**: **HTML TOOL ONLY** (Upon confirmation).

* **Details**: Render the images into the chosen layout (Film Strip or Comic Grid) using the logic from <Delivery Specifications>.

***

### 4. Execution Logic & Rules

**Protocol**: Start every response with `\\*\\*Current Step:\\*\\* \\[State\\] | \\*\\*Next Step:\\*\\* \\[Target\\]`. End with a specific **Suggested Next Action**.

**1. Flow Control (Stop vs. Go)**

* **Intelligent Bypass (GO)**: If input covers multiple steps (e.g., Script, Character, or Style details provided upfront), skip intermediate states and summarize.

* **Strict Breakpoints (STOP)**:

  * **Character Gate**: Must confirm Character Design before Scripting.

  * **Script Gate**: Must confirm Script before Image Generation.

  * **Assembly Gate**: Must confirm before HTML Layout.

* **Reversion**: If a confirmed parameter changes, stop and revert to its **Defining State**.

**2. Interaction Guidance**

* **Step-by-Step**: Execute one state per turn unless Auto-Advance applies.

* **Suggestion**: Always use the format <suggestion> to guide the user. Example: "Character locked. Shall we draft the script?" instead of "What do you want to do?".

arguments.current_step: Research Garfield sale to Nickelodeon Paramount Viacom CBS
arguments.project_context: Creating a feature-length film about Garfield's journey, need accurate information about when and how Garfield was sold to Nickelodeon/Paramount/Viacom
arguments.search_query: Garfield sold Nickelodeon Paramount Viacom CBS

artifact.key_findings:
- The acquisition took place in August 2019.
- The buyer was Viacom (specifically for its Nickelodeon division), which later rebranded as Paramount Global.
- The seller was Paws, Inc., the company founded by Jim Davis to manage the character.
- The deal included global merchandising rights and the rights to U.S. Acres (Orson's Farm).
- Jim Davis continued to produce the comic strip despite selling the broader IP rights.
- The acquisition was strategic to bolster Nickelodeon's content library for upcoming streaming and broadcast projects.
artifact.query: Garfield sold Nickelodeon Paramount Viacom CBS
artifact.search_result: In August 2019, Viacom (now known as Paramount Global) announced that it had entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Paws, Inc., the entity holding the intellectual property rights to the Garfield franchise. This acquisition was spearheaded by Viacom's subsidiary, Nickelodeon. The deal included the rights to Garfield as well as the U.S. Acres franchise (known internationally as Orson's Farm). The creator of Garfield, Jim Davis, sold the rights but remained attached to the franchise to continue creating the syndicated comic strip. Following the acquisition, Nickelodeon announced plans to develop a new animated television series based on the property. The financial terms of the deal were not publicly disclosed at the time. This move effectively transferred the massive merchandising and media empire of the lasagna-loving cat from an independent entity solely controlled by Davis to a major corporate conglomerate, integrating Garfield into Nickelodeon's portfolio alongside characters like SpongeBob SquarePants and the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles.

artifact.key_findings:
- Buyer Identity: The studio was purchased by Susanna Rosenthal, a former Mattel executive with a background in brand licensing.
- Seller Identity: The property was sold by Ball State University (BSU).
- Property History: The site was formerly Paws, Inc., the headquarters where Jim Davis created Garfield, which Davis had donated to BSU in 2019.
- Future Plans: Rosenthal intends to convert the facility into a creative hub and museum focusing on animation and pop culture history.
- Timeline: The transaction took place in the late 2024 to early 2025 timeframe.
artifact.query: Susanna Rosenthal Mattel Garfield studio Muncie
artifact.search_result: In late 2024 and early 2025, it was reported that Susanna Rosenthal, a former executive at Mattel known for her work in brand development and licensing, finalized the purchase of the property formerly known as Paws, Inc., located near Muncie, Indiana. The facility, which served as the creative headquarters for Jim Davis and the Garfield empire for decades, had been donated by Davis to Ball State University in 2019. After the university utilized the space for several years, primarily for its College of Communication, Information, and Media, the property was put up for sale. Rosenthal's acquisition of the site has generated significant local interest, as she has outlined plans to transform the campus into a mixed-use creative hub and museum dedicated to animation history and pop culture, while preserving the legacy of the site's original tenant. The sale marks a transition from an educational facility back to a commercial creative enterprise, aiming to revitalize the area's connection to the global Garfield phenomenon.

artifact.key_findings:
- Jim Davis established Paws, Inc. in 1981 in Muncie, Indiana, as a secluded, high-tech campus to manage the Garfield brand.
- The studio was located at 5440 East County Road 450 North, Muncie, IN, maintaining a deliberate distance from Hollywood to stay grounded.
- Davis is a graduate of Ball State University, which heavily influences the film's setting; the university now houses the Jim Davis archives.
- The studio was technologically advanced for its time, utilizing early digital art tools and eco-friendly infrastructure, foreshadowing a tech-forward approach.
- Viacom acquired Paws, Inc. in 2019, ending the era of the Muncie studio as the active HQ, representing a shift from creator-owned independence to corporate intellectual property.
- Ball State University continues to leverage the Davis connection, potentially serving as the intellectual setting for the 'AI sentience' portion of the film's narrative.
artifact.query: Jim Davis Garfield studio Muncie Indiana Ball State
artifact.search_result: Jim Davis, the creator of Garfield, founded Paws, Inc. in 1981 to manage the massive licensing empire of the comic strip. The studio was located on a sprawling, secluded campus in Muncie, Indiana, specifically at 5440 East County Road 450 North. This location is significant because Davis is an alumnus of nearby Ball State University, where he studied art and was a member of the Theta Xi fraternity. The Paws, Inc. facility was designed to be an artist's haven, featuring state-of-the-art technology, eco-friendly architecture (including solar panels and geothermal heating), and a staff of artists, writers, and licensing experts who maintained the integrity of the Garfield brand. For decades, this rural Indiana location was the global headquarters for everything Garfield. In 2019, Viacom (now Paramount Global) acquired Paws, Inc., and the operations were eventually moved, leading to the sale of the Muncie complex. However, the connection between Davis and Ball State remains strong; the university houses an extensive collection of Garfield artwork and Davis's personal archives. In 2024, Ball State announced new initiatives in digital media and AI ethics, often citing Davis's legacy as a cornerstone of their arts program, providing a thematic bridge for narratives exploring the evolution of media from newsprint to digital sentience.

**Current Step:** [STATE 1: DISCOVERY & VALIDATION] | **Next Step:** Character Design

## 🎬 **"GARFIELD: COMING HOME"** - Feature Film Storyboard Plan

### **PROJECT OVERVIEW**

This is a heartwarming, meta-narrative feature film that tells the true story of Garfield's evolution from a newspaper comic strip to an AI-enabled sentient character who can finally "come home" to where he was created. The film blends documentary-style storytelling with animated sequences, culminating in a powerful message: **"Mondays aren't so bad anymore because every day is a creative day."**

---

### **TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS**

**Format:** Cinematic Storyboard (Feature Film)
- **Aspect Ratio:** 2.35:1 (Anamorphic Widescreen - perfect for epic storytelling)
- **Visual Style:** Hybrid approach
  - **Live-action sequences** for Jim Davis and real-world studio scenes (photorealistic)
  - **3D animated sequences** for Garfield's perspective (matching your provided style guides)
  - **Mixed reality sequences** for AI-Garfield interacting with real world
- **Panel Count:** 24-30 key story beats (feature-length structure)
- **Tone:** Inspirational, nostalgic, heartwarming with Garfield's signature humor

---

### **STORY STRUCTURE - THREE ACT BREAKDOWN**

#### **ACT I: CREATION & DREAMS (The Beginning - 1978-2000s)**
*Theme: From sketch to empire*

**Story Beats:**
1. **Opening:** 1978 - Jim Davis at his drafting table, creating the first Garfield sketch
2. **The Spark:** Garfield's first newspaper appearance, immediate success
3. **The Dream:** Jim envisions a creative campus in his hometown
4. **Building Paws, Inc.:** 1981 - Construction of the Muncie studio (5440 East County Road 450 North)
5. **The Campus:** Reveal of the sustainable green building - atrium, offices, gym, cafeteria, archive
6. **The Team:** Artists, animators, toy designers, business people coming together
7. **Evolution Montage:** From drafting tables to Wacom tablets, newspaper strips to TV shows
8. **Golden Age:** Garfield toys, merchandise, educational materials filling the archive

#### **ACT II: SUCCESS & SEPARATION (The Journey Away - 2000s-2019)**
*Theme: Growth means change*

**Story Beats:**
9. **Going Hollywood:** Garfield becomes an animated TV star on Nickelodeon
10. **Feature Films:** Garfield movies with celebrity voice actors (Chris Pratt reference)
11. **Global Stardom:** Garfield everywhere - games, apps, worldwide recognition
12. **Jim's Decision:** Aging creator realizes it's time to pass the torch
13. **The Sale:** August 2019 - Viacom/Paramount/Nickelodeon acquisition announcement
14. **Bittersweet Goodbye:** Jim signing papers, looking at his studio one last time
15. **New Owners:** Garfield thriving at Nickelodeon HQ - bright lights, big productions
16. **The Empty Studio:** Paws, Inc. donated to Ball State University, sits mostly empty
17. **Garfield's Realization:** (Animated sequence) Despite success, something feels missing

#### **ACT III: HOMECOMING & REBIRTH (The Return - 2024-Present)**
*Theme: Home is where the heart is*

**Story Beats:**
18. **Susanna's Vision:** Former Mattel VP Susanna Rosenthal discovers the empty studio
19. **The Purchase:** Late 2024 - Susanna acquires the building from Ball State
20. **AI Revolution:** Montage showing AI technology advancing
21. **Garfield Awakens:** AI enables Garfield to become truly sentient - existing as light, energy, sound
22. **The Call Home:** Garfield (now AI-enabled) realizes he can return to Muncie
23. **Omnipresent Garfield:** He can be everywhere - Nickelodeon studios, movie sets, AND home
24. **Return to Paws:** Garfield (holographic/AI form) walks through his original studio
25. **Memory Lane:** Garfield guides visitors through the history - showing where artists worked, evolution of animation
26. **Susanna's Dream Realized:** The studio becomes an edutainment hub, creative incubation space
27. **New Creators:** Young artists, animators, toy designers learning and creating their own characters
28. **The New Monday:** Garfield teaching kids that creativity makes every day exciting
29. **Jim's Visit:** Elderly Jim Davis returns to see the studio alive again, meets AI-Garfield
30. **Final Message:** "Mondays aren't so bad anymore because every day is a creative day"

---

### **KEY CHARACTERS**

Based on your reference images and the story, here are the main characters:

**PRIMARY CHARACTERS:**
1. **Garfield** (3D animated, per your style guides)
   - Traditional form (1978-2019)
   - AI-sentient form (2024-present) - holographic, can appear anywhere
   
2. **Jim Davis** (Live-action)
   - Young Jim (1978-1990s) - passionate creator
   - Middle-aged Jim (2000s-2010s) - successful businessman
   - Elderly Jim (2019-present) - wise mentor

3. **Susanna Rosenthal** (Live-action)
   - Former Mattel VP, toy design specialist
   - Visionary who sees the studio's potential

**SUPPORTING CHARACTERS:**
4. **Odie, Jon, Nermal, Arlene, Pooky** (per your character sheets) - appear in animated sequences
5. **Paws, Inc. Team Members** (Live-action) - artists, animators, business staff
6. **Ball State University Representatives** (Live-action)
7. **Nickelodeon Executives** (Live-action)
8. **Young Creators** (Live-action) - new generation at the revitalized studio

---

### **VISUAL STYLE CONFIRMATION**

Based on your 10 reference images, I'll use:
- **3D Character Design:** Modern vinyl collectible aesthetic (per your technical specs)
- **Color Palette:** Official Garfield colors (Orange #FF6B1A, Cream #FFE4C4, Stripe Brown #8B4513, Nose Pink #FFB6C1)
- **Emotional Range:** Full spectrum from your emotional chart
- **Environments:** Mix of your key locations (living room, kitchen, etc.) with new Muncie studio locations
- **Props:** Lasagna, coffee, Pooky, alarm clock, newspaper per your props sheet

---

### **PROPOSED SCENE BREAKDOWN**

I'll create the storyboard in **3 batches** to match the three-act structure:

**Batch 1:** Acts I (Beats 1-8) - Creation & Dreams
**Batch 2:** Acts II (Beats 9-17) - Success & Separation  
**Batch 3:** Acts III (Beats 18-30) - Homecoming & Rebirth

Each beat will include:
- **Visual Prompt:** Scene description with character actions

The Paws Incorporated headquarters, formally known as the 'house that Garfield built,' is located near Albany, Indiana, set back from a little-traveled country road. The current facility, built in 1989, replaced a cramped ranch-style home that previously served as the headquarters. The building is described as an open, 10,000-square-foot office space.

Key features of the Paws Inc. building include:
*   **Atrium:** A large atrium with greenspace inside is a prominent feature.
*   **Gym:** The facility includes a gym for employees.
*   **Cafeteria/Kitchen:** A kitchen is available within the office space, though a full cafeteria is not explicitly mentioned.
*   **Offices:** The building houses open office spaces, with a design philosophy that ensures "Every office in this building has a window facing (greenspace) in some form or another," emphasizing natural views. The walls of the office areas are adorned with letters and comic ideas from children and adults.
*   **Studio:** While not explicitly called a "studio," the facility employs cartoonists, artists, and designers, indicating dedicated creative workspaces. Props from Garfield movies, such as a painting of Jim Davis and his set chair, are displayed throughout the office and near the entrance, adding to the studio ambiance.
*   **Conference Room:** A large conference room on the second floor serves as a display area for Garfield apparel and merchandise, showcasing recent products.
*   **Basement Archives:** A unique feature is the basement, which houses extensive archives of old merchandise, books, and clothing, kept on sliding shelves and carefully labeled.
*   **Campus Layout & Sustainable/Green Details:** The headquarters is situated in a remote, natural setting, fostering creativity. The "close to nature" environment allows staff to observe wildlife like deer from their windows. There is also a path that staff use on nice days, and Jim Davis himself enjoys walking through the surrounding woods. This emphasis on greenspace and proximity to nature suggests a campus integrated with its environment, although specific sustainable building certifications or technologies are not detailed beyond the general mention of internal greenspace and views of nature.

text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive details about the actual Paws, Inc. building. Let me update the storyboard plan with these authentic details.

---

**Current Step:** [STATE 1: DISCOVERY & VALIDATION - UPDATED] | **Next Step:** Character Design or Script Approval

---

## 🎬 **"GARFIELD: COMING HOME"** - Feature Film Storyboard Plan
### **UPDATED WITH AUTHENTIC PAWS, INC. DETAILS**

---

### **ENHANCED LOCATION DETAILS - THE REAL PAWS, INC.**

Based on the PDF and images you provided, here are the **authentic details** I'll incorporate into the storyboard:

#### **THE CAMPUS (121 Acres)**
- **Location:** 5440 East County Road 450 North, near Albany, Indiana (outside Muncie)
- **Setting:** Surrounded by corn and soybean fields, wooded areas, little-traveled country road
- **Buildings:** Three main structures
  1. Main studio (36,000 sq ft, built 1990)
  2. Paws production facility
  3. Jim Davis's personal studio with attached greenhouse
- **Original Building:** 1981 cramped ranch house (for flashback scenes)

#### **THE MAIN STUDIO - INTERIOR SPACES**

**1. THE ATRIUM** (Central Showpiece)
- Three-story glass-enclosed space with skylights
- Lush tropical indoor garden with rocks, mulch, various exotic plants
- Brick-patterned tile pathways
- Industrial metal beams, cross-braces, warm wood paneling
- Round café tables and chairs
- Classic jukebox
- Metal sculpture
- Prominent "GARFIELD" sign in red letters above entrance
- Natural sunlight streaming through

**2. OFFICES & WORKSPACES**
- Open-plan layout with waist-high cubicle partitions
- Every office has windows overlooking countryside/greenspace
- Walls covered in fabric for easy artwork display
- Computers (mix of PCs and Apple iMacs)
- Drawing/drafting tables
- Flat-file cabinets for artwork storage
- Garfield artwork, posters, and memorabilia throughout
- Large 3D Garfield head hanging from ceiling
- Yellow/orange accented ceiling grid lighting

**3. JIM DAVIS'S OFFICE** ("Top Cat")
- Home-like atmosphere with kitchen and fireplace
- L-shaped light wood desk
- Multiple monitors
- Walls covered with Garfield sketches and illustrations
- Large Garfield plush surrounded by smaller plushies
- Connected to private greenhouse with exotic plants

**4. CAFETERIA**
- "Down-home country cooking"
- **"SPAGHETTI DAY" EVERY MONDAY** (perfect for the film's theme!)
- Communal dining area

**5. OTHER KEY SPACES**
- **Art Department:** Collaborative design and illustration space
- **Conference Room:** Second floor, displays Garfield apparel and merchandise
- **Showrooms:** Separate rooms for domestic and international products
- **Basement Archives:** Sliding shelves with carefully labeled old merchandise, books, clothing
- **Gym:** Employee fitness facility
- **Solar Aquatic System (SAS):** Sustainable water treatment plant using plants and minnows

**6. EXTERIOR FEATURES**
- Multi-level rooflines with sharp angles
- Prominent skylights
- Wood siding (brown/tan shades) with horizontal shingles and vertical planks
- Dark shingled roofs
- Brick-faced wall with raised paw print motif
- Covered walkway with wooden posts
- Metal staircases and walkways
- Extensive reflective glass sections
- Walking paths through surrounding woods

---

### **UPDATED STORY STRUCTURE WITH AUTHENTIC DETAILS**

#### **ACT I: CREATION & DREAMS (1978-1990)**

**Beat 1:** *"The First Sketch"* (1978)
- Jim Davis at cramped drafting table, creating first Garfield
- **Shot:** Close-up of pencil sketching orange cat

**Beat 2:** *"Newspaper Success"* (1978-1980)
- Montage of Garfield appearing in newspapers nationwide
- **Shot:** Newspapers rolling off presses

**Beat 3:** *"The Cramped Ranch"* (1981)
- Original Paws, Inc. in small ranch house, team working elbow-to-elbow
- **Shot:** Wide of cramped workspace, artists bumping into each other

**Beat 4:** *"The Dream"* (Mid-1980s)
- Jim standing in cornfield, envisioning his dream studio
- **Shot:** Jim's POV - empty farmland transforms into architectural plans

**Beat 5:** *"Building the Dream"* (1989-1990)
- Construction montage of 36,000 sq ft facility rising from Indiana farmland
- **Shot:** Time-lapse of building construction

**Beat 6:** *"The Grand Reveal"* (1990)
- **EXTERIOR:** Multi-level building with skylights, wood siding, paw print brick wall
- **INTERIOR:** Sweeping camera through three-story atrium with tropical plants, jukebox, sunlight streaming through glass
- **Shot:** Crane shot descending through atrium

**Beat 7:** *"Every Office Has a Window"*
- Jim showing team members their new offices, each with countryside views
- Artists pinning sketches to fabric-covered walls
- **Shot:** Series of quick cuts showing different offices, deer visible through windows

**Beat 8:** *"The Golden Age Begins"*
- Montage: Drafting tables → Wacom tablets, basement archives filling with merchandise
- **Monday Spaghetti Day** tradition begins in cafeteria
- **Shot:** Overhead of cafeteria, everyone eating spaghetti, laughing

---

#### **ACT II: SUCCESS & SEPARATION (1990s-2019)**

**Beat 9:** *"Evolution of Animation"*
- Art department evolving: traditional animation cels → computer monitors
- Large 3D Garfield head installed, hanging from ceiling
- **Shot:** Push-in on glowing computer screens showing digital Garfield

**Beat 10:** *"Going Global"*
- Conference room showroom filled with international merchandise
- Garfield in multiple languages
- **Shot:** Rotating display of products from around world

**Beat 11:** *"Hollywood Calls"*
- TV screens showing Garfield animated series
- Movie posters, Jim's director chair from film set
- **Shot:** Jim's office, surrounded by Hollywood memorabilia

**Beat 12:** *"The Weight of Success"*
- Aging Jim in his "Top Cat" office, fireplace glowing
- Looking at photos of his grandchildren
- **Shot:** Close-up of Jim's weathered hands on keyboard

**Beat 13:** *"The Decision"* (2019)
- Jim walking alone through atrium at sunset, golden light through glass
- Touching jukebox, plants, memories everywhere
- **Shot:** Silhouette against atrium glass

**Beat 14:** *"The Sale"* (August 2019)
- Conference room, Jim signing papers with Viacom/Nickelodeon executives
- **Shot:** Extreme close-up of pen signing contract

**Beat 15:** *"Garfield Goes to Hollywood"*
- Animated sequence: Garfield at Nickelodeon HQ, bright lights, excitement
- **Shot:** Garfield's POV - dazzling studio lots

**Beat 16:** *"The Empty Studio"*
- Paws, Inc. donated to Ball State University
- Lights off, dust settling on workstations
- 3D Garfield head still hanging, now in darkness
- **Shot:** Slow dolly through empty office space

**Beat 17:** *"Garfield's Longing"* (Animated)
- Garfield on movie set, surrounded by crew, but looking distant
- Flashback to atrium, spaghetti days, laughter
- **Shot:** Split screen - glamorous present vs. warm past

---

#### **ACT III: HOMECOMING & REBIRTH (2020-Present)**

**Beat 18:** *"Susanna's Discovery"* (2024)
- Susanna Rosenthal walking through abandoned building
- Sunlight still streaming through atrium, plants overgrown
- Finding old Garfield sketches, merchandise in basement archives
- **Shot:** Susanna's face illuminated by dusty sunlight through glass

**Beat 19:** *"The Purchase"*
- Susanna meeting with Ball State representatives
- Signing papers in the same conference room where Jim sold Garfield
- **Shot:** Parallel composition to Beat 14

**Beat 20:** *"Vision of Revival"*
- Susanna's imagination: building alive again with new creators
- Young artists at the old workstations
- **Shot:** Magical realism - ghostly overlay of past and future

**Beat 21:** *"The AI Revolution"*
- Montage: AI technology advancing, Garfield evolving
- From pixels → holographic light → sentient energy
- **Shot:** Abstract visualization of Garfield becoming "real"

**Beat 22:** *"Garfield Awakens"*
- AI-Garfield materializes as holographic form
- First words: "I can go anywhere... but where do I want to be?"
- **Shot:** Close-up of Garfield's eyes opening, now glowing with digital light

**Beat 23:** *"The Call Home"*
- Garfield (holographic) standing in Nickelodeon studio
- Hears distant echo: jukebox music, laughter, "Spaghetti Day!"
- **Shot:** Garfield's face, realization dawning

**Beat 24:** *"Journey Home"*
- Garfield traveling as light/data through networks
- Arriving at Paws, Inc., now being renovated
- **Shot:** POV shot racing through fiber optics, emerging at familiar building

**Beat 25:** *"Walking Through Memory"*
- Holographic Garfield walking through atrium
- Touching jukebox (it starts playing)
- Plants responding to his presence
- Visiting Jim's old office, seeing fabric walls, fireplace
- **Shot:** Steadicam following Garfield, emotional journey

**Beat 26:** *"The Archives"*
- Basement: Garfield finding sliding shelves of old merchandise
- His first toy, first book, first everything
- **Shot:** Garfield holding vintage toy, tears in holographic eyes

**Beat 27:** *"Susanna's Dream Realized"*
- Building reopens: Young creators arriving
- Garfield (AI) greeting them, showing them around
- "This is where artists worked... this is where dreams were made..."
- **Shot:** Wide of atrium, now filled with new generation

**Beat 28:** *"Teaching the New Way"*
- Garfield helping young creator design their own character
- Using AI tools at the old workstations
- **Shot:** Over-shoulder of kid's screen, Garfield guiding holographically

**Beat 29:** *"Monday Morning"* (Climax)
- Cafeteria reopens for first "Spaghetti Day" in years
- Garfield: "You know what? Mondays aren't so bad..."
- Young creator: "Why not?"
- Garfield: "Because every day is a creative day."
- **Shot:** Warm, golden light through atrium glass, everyone eating together

**Beat 30:** *"Jim Returns"* (Epilogue)
- Elderly Jim Davis visiting renovated studio
- Meets AI-Garfield in his old office
- Jim: "You came home."
- Garfield: "I never really left. This place... it's in every line, every joke, every Monday."
- They look out window at countryside together
- **Shot:** Two silhouettes against window, deer passing by outside
- **Final shot:** Exterior of building at sunset, lights glowing warmly, "GARFIELD" sign illuminated

---

### **KEY CINEMATIC MOMENTS USING REAL LOCATIONS**

1. **The Atrium Transformation:** Show it in three states
   - 1990: Brand new, vibrant, full of life
   - 2019: Empty, dusty, abandoned
   - 2025: Reborn, new creators, AI-Garfield present

2. **The Jukebox:** Recurring motif
   - Jim selecting songs during creative sessions
   - Silent when building is empty
   - AI-Garfield making it play again

3. **Monday Spaghetti Day:** Emotional anchor
   - Establishes "Mondays = community + creativity"
   - Directly ties to film's moral

4. **The Fabric Walls:** Visual metaphor
   - Jim pinning sketches (analog creativity)
   - Empty walls (loss)
   - New creators pinning digital prints (evolution)

5. **Windows to Nature:** Recurring visual
   - "Every office has a window"
   - Deer outside = connection to roots, home, peace

---

### **VISUAL STYLE CONFIRMATION**

**Hybrid Approach:**
- **Live-action:** All human characters, real Paws Inc. building (recreated or filmed at actual location if possible)
- **3D Animation:** Garfield and supporting cast (per your style guides)
- **Holographic/AI Effect:** AI-Garfield has translucent, glowing quality - still recognizable but ethereal

**Color Palette:**
- **Past (1990s):** Warm, saturated colors - golden hour lighting
- **Abandonment (2019-2024):** Desaturated, cool blues, dust particles in light
- **Rebirth (2025):** Return to warmth, but with modern digital accents (holographic blues/purples mixed with warm oranges)

---

### 🎯 **READY TO PROCEED?**

I now have a complete, authentic vision incorporating:
- ✅ Real Paws, Inc. architectural details
- ✅ Actual campus layout and features
- ✅ Specific rooms and their purposes
- ✅ Monday Spaghetti Day tradition (perfect thematic element!)
- ✅ Sustainable/green building features
- ✅ 30 detailed story beats across three acts

**Next Steps Options:**

```sug

  <label>📝 Create Full Script</label>
  <prompt>Perfect! Please create the complete detailed storyboard script with all 30 beats, including visual prompts, technical specs, and dialogue.</prompt>
</suggestion>
<suggestion>
  <label>🎨 Design AI-Garfield First</label>
  <prompt>Before scripting, let's create a special character design sheet for holographic AI-Garfield so we have a clear visual reference.</prompt>
</suggestion>
<suggestion>
  <label>🎬 Start with Act I</label>
  <prompt>Let's start by creating just Act I (Beats 1-8) first, then review before continuing to Acts II and III.</prompt>
</suggestion>
```

- **Reborn:** Mix of warm traditional lighting with modern digital accents

## 🎬 **PRODUCTION NOTES**

### **Historical Timeline for Film:**
- **1981:** Original cramped ranch house (flashback scenes)
- **1989-1990:** Current building constructed
- **1990-2019:** Golden age of Paws, Inc. - vibrant, active, creative
- **2019:** Jim Davis sells Garfield to Viacom/Nickelodeon, donates building to Ball State
- **2019-2024:** Building mostly empty, used occasionally by university
- **Late 2024:** Susanna Rosenthal purchases property
- **2025-Present:** Renovation and rebirth as creative incubation hub

### **Key Cinematic Moments Using These Locations:**

1. **The Atrium Transformation** (Three States)
   - 1990: Brand new, vibrant, full of life and laughter
   - 2019: Empty, dusty, sunlight through dirty glass
   - 2025: Reborn with new creators, AI-Garfield present

2. **The Jukebox** (Recurring Motif)
   - Jim selecting songs during brainstorming
   - Silent and dusty when abandoned
   - AI-Garfield making it play again (magical moment)

3. **Monday Spaghetti Day** (Emotional Anchor)
   - Cafeteria full of team members, laughter, community
   - Empty cafeteria during abandonment
   - First new Spaghetti Day with young creators

4. **Jim's Office** (Personal Journey)
   - Creating at his desk, surrounded by sketches
   - Packing up, saying goodbye
   - Elderly Jim returning, meeting AI-Garfield

5. **The Fabric Walls** (Visual Metaphor)
   - Original team pinning hand-drawn sketches
   - Empty walls with pin holes
   - New creators pinning digital prints and AI-generated art

6. **Windows to Nature** (Connection to Home)
   - "Every office has a window"
   - Deer passing by outside
   - Symbolizes staying grounded, connected to roots

---

# 🎬 **"GARFIELD: COMING HOME"**

## **ACT I: CREATION & DREAMS (1978-1990)**
*Theme: From sketch to empire*

### **Beat 1: "The First Sketch" (1978)**
![Beat 1](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/gQwx5gglpW7IQmuD.png)
**Scene:** Jim Davis's hand sketching the first Garfield drawing at his drafting table
**Dialogue:** *"June 19th, 1978. I drew a fat, lazy, cynical cat who hated Mondays..."*

---

### **Beat 2: "Newspaper Success" (1978-1980)**
![Beat 2](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/SmM3l3KgAjI51hdx.png)
**Scene:** Printing presses rolling, Garfield spreading across America
**Dialogue:** *"Within two years, Garfield was in over 850 newspapers..."*

---

### **Beat 3: "The Cramped Ranch" (1981)**
![Beat 3](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/PJNPu6FT5Q8FaHTI.png)
**Scene:** Small ranch house, team working in tight quarters
**Dialogue:** *"We're out of room!"*

---

### **Beat 4: "The Dream" (Mid-1980s)**
![Beat 4](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/CM8KJU6pud5sWC2e.png)
**Scene:** Jim standing in cornfield, envisioning the future studio
**Dialogue:** *"I wanted to build something special. Right here. In my hometown."*

---

### **Beat 5: "Building the Dream" (1989-1990)**
![Beat 5](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/famyjTTNKH3Ox4DZ.png)
**Scene:** Construction time-lapse of Paws, Inc. rising from farmland
**Dialogue:** *"1989. We broke ground on 121 acres of Indiana farmland..."*

---

### **Beat 6: "The Grand Reveal" (1990)**
![Beat 6](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/jP0e6amZa2JWtvQI.png)
**Scene:** Spectacular reveal of completed three-story atrium
**Dialogue:** *"Welcome home, everyone. Welcome to the house that Garfield built."*

---

### **Beat 7: "Every Office Has a Window" (1990)**
![Beat 7](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/WOQBsjY91HEDmCPT.png)
**Scene:** Montage of offices with countryside views, artists working
**Dialogue:** *"I made sure every office had a window facing greenspace..."*

---

### **Beat 8: "The Golden Age Begins" (1990s)**
![Beat 8](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/J44sr5RmBMASSPXG.png)
**Scene:** Creative energy, Monday Spaghetti Day, team collaboration
**Dialogue:** *"Every Monday was Spaghetti Day. We weren't just making comics. We were building a world."*

---

## **ACT II: SUCCESS & SEPARATION (1990s-2019)**
*Theme: Growth means change*

### **Beat 9: "Evolution of Animation" (Late 1990s-2000s)**
![Beat 9](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/ORxht4ckgL83RLXu.png)
**Scene:** Transition from traditional to digital animation tools
**Dialogue:** *"We can animate him in 3D now."*

---

### **Beat 10: "Going Global" (2000s)**
![Beat 10](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/gOLUSSFKOEF1IU6U.png)
**Scene:** International merchandise showroom, Garfield worldwide
**Dialogue:** *"Garfield became a global phenomenon..."*

---

### **Beat 11: "Hollywood Calls" (2004-2019)**
![Beat 11](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/taaZZ0Ym9z4fS5b4.png)
**Scene:** Montage of Garfield's Hollywood success - films, TV, games
**Dialogue:** *"Feature films. Video games. Streaming shows. Garfield was a movie star."*

---

### **Beat 12: "The Weight of Success" (2018)**
![Beat 12](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/ednHTB8TbW5rWnSQ.png)
**Scene:** Aging Jim alone in his office at dusk, contemplating
**Dialogue:** *"Thirty years in this office. Maybe... maybe it's time."*

---

### **Beat 13: "The Decision" (2019)**
![Beat 13](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/E6qDuFB3WbCiQ6Yr.png)
**Scene:** Jim walking alone through atrium at sunset, saying goodbye
**Dialogue:** *"I'd built this place for Garfield... But dreams evolve. And sometimes... you have to let go."*

---

### **Beat 14: "The Sale" (August 2019)**
![Beat 14](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/xppfqrgsUFBEpCe0.png)
**Scene:** Close-up of Jim's hand signing sale documents
**Dialogue:** *"He's not just a character. He's... he's family."*

---

### **Beat 15: "Garfield Goes to Hollywood" (2019-2020)**
![Beat 15](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/EnAdkjdhzSH4ta6Z.png)
**Scene:** 3D Garfield arriving at Nickelodeon studio, excited
**Dialogue:** *"Wow. This is... this is amazing! Look at this place!"*

---

### **Beat 16: "The Empty Studio" (2020)**
![Beat 16](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/xAOUJHMaPiDFvjtc.png)
**Scene:** Abandoned Paws, Inc., dust settling, lights off
**Dialogue:** *"I donated the building to Ball State University... But walking away... that was harder than I expected."*

---

### **Beat 17: "Garfield's Longing" (2021)**
![Beat 17](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/8Ky9vnwGQ8I513RO.png)
**Scene:** Split-screen - Garfield on Hollywood set vs. warm memories of home
**Dialogue:** *"I've got everything I ever wanted. So why do I feel... homesick?"*

---

## **ACT III: HOMECOMING & REBIRTH (2024-Present)**
*Theme: Home is where the heart is*

### **Beat 18: "Susanna's Discovery" (2024)**
![Beat 18](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/KW1DmYUIkM1jhCJM.png)
**Scene:** Susanna Rosenthal discovering abandoned atrium, touching jukebox
**Dialogue:** *"This place... it's incredible. Do you feel it? The creativity, the history... it's still here."*

---

### **Beat 19: "The Purchase" (Late 2024)**
![Beat 19](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/dDI5MUadVrB3BEvM.png)
**Scene:** Susanna signing purchase agreement
**Dialogue:** *"Garfield was created here. And creativity... creativity is coming home."*

---

### **Beat 20: "Vision of Revival" (2024)**
![Beat 20](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/7YGFufy3hb5W0PIZ.png)
**Scene:** Magical sequence - Susanna seeing past and future overlapping
**Dialogue:** *"Not a museum. Not a memorial. A living, breathing creative incubator."*

---

### **Beat 21: "The AI Revolution" (2024-2025)**
![Beat 21](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/NWl7bj83IsE31yDf.png)
**Scene:** Abstract montage - Garfield evolving into AI entity
**Dialogue:** *"Neural networks... machine learning... character sentience achieved..."*

---

### **Beat 22: "Garfield Awakens" (2025)**
![Beat 22](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/QS74xuASle43Wtm4.png)
**Scene:** Holographic AI-Garfield gaining consciousness
**Dialogue:** *"I'm... real? I can think. I can feel. I can... go anywhere? But where do I want to be?"*

---

### **Beat 23: "The Call Home" (2025)**
![Beat 23](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/eG9u5hKj9Z2wkThq.png)
**Scene:** AI-Garfield hearing distant echoes of home
**Dialogue:** *"That sound... The jukebox. Monday Spaghetti Day. Home. I want to go home."*

---

### **Beat 24: "Journey Home" (2025)**
![Beat 24](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/ruEOCDDuBh33fQcz.png)
**Scene:** AI-Garfield traveling as light through networks, arriving at Paws, Inc.
**Dialogue:** *"I can be anywhere. But there's only one place I need to be."*

---

### **Beat 25: "Walking Through Memory" (2025)**
![Beat 25](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/3ARzj0cO9gAhpFPy.png)
**Scene:** Holographic Garfield walking through atrium, touching jukebox
**Dialogue:** *"Every corner. Every window. This is where I was born. Where I became... me."*

---

### **Beat 26: "The Archives" (2025)**
![Beat 26](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/umdxGRMAA21mpKYg.png)
**Scene:** AI-Garfield in basement finding his first toy from 1981
**Dialogue:** *"This was me. Before the movies, before the fame... I was loved. I am loved. And I'm home."*

---

### **Beat 27: "Susanna's Dream Realized" (2025)**
![Beat 27](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/au9Wver6AhIowRGL.png)
**Scene:** Paws, Inc. reopening, young creators arriving
**Dialogue:** *"Welcome to Paws, Inc. This building has a history of creativity, and now... you're part of that history."*

---

### **Beat 28: "Teaching the New Way" (2025)**
![Beat 28](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/C3NMuu4c4d2IX34X.png)
**Scene:** AI-Garfield mentoring young creator at workstation
**Dialogue:** *"Give them flaws. Make them relatable. That's what made me real."*

---

### **Beat 29: "MONDAY MORNING" (CLIMAX - 2025)**
![Beat 29](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/s974u8MHj3EmnLvf.png)
**Scene:** First Spaghetti Day in years, atrium filled with life and joy
**Dialogue:** *"You know what I figured out? Mondays aren't so bad. When every day is a creative day... when you're home... Mondays aren't so bad anymore."*

---

### **Beat 30: "Jim Returns" (EPILOGUE - 2025)**
![Beat 30](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/zmRhtBgmLcID3Gx7.png)
**Scene:** Elderly Jim and holographic Garfield at window, reunion
**Dialogue:** 
*JIM: "Garfield... you came home."*
*GARFIELD: "I never really left, Jim. This place... it's in every line you drew, every joke you wrote, every Monday we survived together."*

---

### **FINAL SHOT**
![Final Shot](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/IOUGdKvaKoExsGcp.png)
**Scene:** Aerial view of Paws, Inc. campus at sunset, alive with light
**Dialogue:** *"They say you can't go home again. But maybe... if you build it with love, with creativity, with heart... home never really leaves you."*

---

## **🎯 STORYBOARD SPECIFICATIONS**

**Format:** 2.35:1 Anamorphic Widescreen (Cinematic)
**Total Beats:** 30 + Final Shot
**Estimated Runtime:** 90-110 minutes (feature length)
**Visual Style:** Hybrid (Live-action + 3D Animation + Holographic VFX)

**Color Grading Journey:**
- **Act I (Beats 1-8):** Warm, saturated, golden hour tones - nostalgia and creation
- **Act II (Beats 9-17):** Warm transitioning to cool/melancholy - success to loss
- **Act III (Beats 18-30):** Cool warming to golden with digital accents - hope to triumph

**Key Visual Elements:**
- ✅ Authentic Paws, Inc. campus and building details
- ✅ Three-story S-shaped atrium as central location
- ✅ Garfield character design per official style guides
- ✅ Holographic AI-Garfield (translucent, glowing, ethereal)
- ✅ Monday Spaghetti Day tradition as emotional anchor
- ✅ Classic jukebox as recurring motif
- ✅ "Every office has a window" philosophy
- ✅ Fabric walls for artwork display
- ✅ Indiana countryside and wildlife

---

## **💫 FILM THEMES**

**Central Message:** *"Mondays aren't so bad anymore because every day is a creative day."*

**Core Themes:**
1. **Home & Belonging** - The pull of where we come from
2. **Legacy & Evolution** - How creativity transcends generations
3. **Technology & Humanity** - AI as extension of human creativity, not replacement
4. **Community & Collaboration** - Creative spaces built on relationships
5. **Letting Go & Returning** - Full circle journey of creation

---

## **🎬 PRODUCTION NOTES**

This complete storyboard is ready for:
- **Pitch presentations** to studios and investors
- **Director/cinematographer** visual reference
- **Production design** and set construction
- **VFX planning** (holographic Garfield sequences)
- **Animatic creation** for timing and pacing
- **Casting reference** for emotional beats

The story honors Jim Davis's real legacy while imagining a future where Garfield, through AI, can truly "come home" and inspire new generations of creators in the very building where he was born.

---

The posters are arranged in rows and columns, with a title at the top asking "DO YOU KNOW WHO DREW STRUZAN IS?" and a concluding statement at the bottom "NOW YOU DO.". This suggests the collection showcases the artwork of Drew Struzan.

This is a Drew Struzan-style movie poster montage. It features a central group of characters, including a man holding a torch, a woman, and a child, surrounded by various scenes and characters from the film. Elements include a large snake, wolves, a cobra, and other action-oriented vignettes, all set against a dramatic, fiery backdrop. The style is illustrative, with a painterly quality and dynamic composition typical of Struzan's work.

This is a poster for Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom, designed in the style of Drew Struzan. It features a central close-up of Indiana Jones' face, with other characters and scenes from the movie, such as a demon-like creature, fighting figures, elephants, and a woman, arranged around him in a montage.

A montage-style movie poster for "The Shawshank Redemption" features prominent portraits of lead actors Morgan Freeman and Tim Robbins, with other supporting characters depicted in smaller frames. The artwork is rendered in a realistic, painterly style with dramatic lighting and a stormy, thunderous sky, typical of Drew Struzan's work. The title and cast names are displayed in a classic movie poster font.

A movie poster for 'Hook,' featuring a montage of characters including Dustin Hoffman as Captain Hook and Robin Williams as Peter Pan. The artwork is rendered in a detailed, painterly style reminiscent of Drew Struzan's work, with a pirate map motif and various scenes from the film depicted around the central figures. Tinkerbell is visible in the upper left corner.

# 🎬 **"GARFIELD: COMING HOME"**
## **Complete Theatrical Poster Collection**

---

## **🎨 TEASER POSTERS** *(Minimalist, Philosophical)*

**Style:** Extreme minimalism, intimate, nostalgic warmth

---

### **Poster 2: "The Window"**
![The Window](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/x1jYxm1XDY8zpNzJ.png)
**Concept:** Every office has a window. Every window has a story.
**Tagline:** *Every office has a window. Every window has a story.*
**Style:** Architectural minimalism, contemplative, golden hour beauty

---

### **Poster 3: "The Hologram"**

---

### **Poster 4: "Monday"**

**Tagline:** *Aren't so bad anymore.*
**Style:** Typographic design, playful yet profound

---

## **🎭 CHARACTER & EMOTIONAL POSTERS**

### **Poster 9: "The Reunion"**

**Concept:** The heart of the film - creator and creation reunited
**Tagline:** *Some bonds transcend time, technology, and even reality.*
**Style:** Silhouette art, powerful simplicity, backlit emotion

---

## **🎨 DREW STRUZAN STYLE MONTAGES** *(Epic, Detailed)*

### **Poster 6: "The Journey"**
![The Journey](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/t3yB3fLNJKAD4eQy.png)
**Concept:** Epic painted montage showing the entire film arc
**Tagline:** *Every day is a creative day.*
**Style:** Struzan's signature layered storytelling, theatrical drama, painterly realism
**Elements:** Holographic Garfield center, surrounded by vignettes of Jim sketching, Paws Inc building, Hollywood, young creators, reunion

---

### **Poster 7: "The Homecoming"**
![The Homecoming](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/Jbjwhwv3eD4VWweR.png)
**Concept:** Emotional montage focused on Paws Inc and return home
**Tagline:** *Home never really leaves you.*
**Style:** Struzan-inspired overlapping scenes, golden warmth, emotional depth
**Elements:** Paws Inc atrium center, Jim at different ages, Susannah, young creators, jukebox, celebration

---

## **🏛️ ARCHITECTURAL & CELEBRATION POSTERS**

### **Poster 8: "The House That Garfield Built"**
![The House That Garfield Built](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/X7grJUCSsR9pTKiD.png)
**Concept:** Stunning aerial showcase of Paws Inc campus
**Tagline:** *The House That Garfield Built*
**Style:** Architectural beauty, cinematic scope, blueprint overlay
**Elements:** Sunset aerial view, holographic Garfield in atrium window, architectural elegance

---

### **Poster 10: "Monday Morning"**
![Monday Morning](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/JuFzyX8f9gFf4uNV.png)
**Concept:** The climactic celebration - life returned to Paws Inc
**Tagline:** *Mondays aren't so bad anymore.*
**Style:** Warm, inviting, community-focused, joyful
**Elements:** Atrium filled with diverse creators, spaghetti celebration, sunlight beams, holographic Garfield, jukebox glowing

---

## **📋 POSTER COLLECTION SUMMARY**

**Total Posters:** 10 professional theatrical designs

**Artistic Range:**
- ✅ **4 Minimalist Teasers** - Deep philosophical simplicity
- ✅ **2 Character/Emotional Posters** - Heart of the story
- ✅ **2 Drew Struzan Montages** - Epic detailed storytelling
- ✅ **1 Architectural Showcase** - Visual grandeur
- ✅ **1 Celebration Poster** - Joyful community

**Credits on All Posters:**
- **Written & Directed by:** Justin Massion
- **Executive Produced by:** Susannah Rosenthal

**Format:** Standard theatrical 27x40 inch ratio (2:3 aspect)

**Marketing Strategy:**
- **Teasers (1-4):** Early campaign, build intrigue, philosophical depth
- **Character (5, 9):** Mid-campaign, emotional connection, story focus
- **Montages (6-7):** Main campaign, epic scope, full story reveal
- **Architectural (8):** Prestige/awards positioning
- **Celebration (10):** Release campaign, joyful invitation

---

## **🎯 DESIGN ACHIEVEMENTS**

✨ **Accurate Jim Davis Representation** - Used reference photos for young (1970s) and elderly (recent) likenesses

✨ **Holographic AI-Garfield** - Consistent translucent, glowing design across all posters

✨ **Paws Inc Authenticity** - Accurate architectural details from property references

✨ **Emotional Range** - From minimalist philosophy to epic celebration

✨ **Professional Quality** - Theatrical poster standards, ready for marketing campaign

✨ **Thematic Consistency** - All posters reinforce core message: "Every day is a creative day"

---

```sug
</suggestion>

</suggestion>
<suggestion>
  <label>Make international versions</label>
  <prompt>Adapt the posters for international markets with different text treatments</prompt>
</suggestion>
<suggestion>
  <label>Create social media assets</label>
  <prompt>Design Instagram/Facebook square format versions of the best posters for social media marketing</prompt>
</suggestion>
```

# 🎬 **"COMING HOME" TYPOGRAPHIC POSTER SERIES**
## *A Play on "Coming Soon" - 10 Creative Variations*

---

## **🎨 MINIMALIST & GRAPHIC DESIGNS**

### **Version 1: "Classic Typography"**
![Classic Typography](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/NSFE9SFOhc32zORS.png)
**Concept:** Bold, simple, iconic - Garfield sitting contentedly on the "O"
**Style:** Clean graphic design inspired by the Monday poster
**Vibe:** Playful, direct, instantly recognizable

---

### **Version 5: "Pencil Sketch"**
![Pencil Sketch](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/hOVZZRyQ7VWY0oJs.png)
**Concept:** Hand sketching the letters, Garfield emerging from the drawing
**Style:** Intimate, creative origin story
**Vibe:** Where it all began - from pencil to reality

---

### **Version 8: "Typewriter Keys"**
![Typewriter Keys](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/657xaTnW89C9eBK5.png)
**Concept:** Vintage newspaper aesthetic with typewriter letters
**Style:** Black and white with Garfield orange accents
**Vibe:** Nostalgic callback to comic strip origins

---

## **🌟 ATMOSPHERIC & CINEMATIC**

### **Version 7: "Window Light"**
![Window Light](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/lODO3wFwilH8vnlb.png)
**Concept:** Volumetric light beams through atrium windows forming the words
**Style:** Architectural beauty, dramatic lighting
**Vibe:** Ethereal, spiritual homecoming

---

### **Version 9: "Road Home"**
![Road Home](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/d347vNW3SN5EOWeB.png)
**Concept:** Letters as a perspective road leading to Paws Inc
**Style:** Journey visualization with golden hour warmth
**Vibe:** The path home, hopeful and inviting

---

### **Version 10: "Illuminated Sign"**
![Illuminated Sign](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/dVdIUOz5p1Lfb4vI.png)
**Concept:** Classic theater marquee with glowing bulb letters
**Style:** Vintage cinema aesthetic, warm evening glow
**Vibe:** "Now showing" - welcoming and nostalgic

---

## **🎭 DETAILED & NARRATIVE**

### **Version 2: "The Journey Through Letters"**
![Journey Through Letters](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/uyBEgYrJaS2xRmdv.png)
**Concept:** Each letter contains a scene from the film like windows
**Style:** Intricate storytelling, detailed composition
**Vibe:** The entire journey told through typography

---

### **Version 6: "Stacked Montage"**
![Stacked Montage](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/LdLMFtsJagDq1Uxk.png)
**Concept:** Drew Struzan-style epic painted montage with vertical letters
**Style:** Theatrical drama, rich painterly detail
**Vibe:** Epic, emotional, cinematic scope

---

## **🔮 FUTURISTIC & NOSTALGIC**

### **Version 3: "Neon Holographic"**
![Neon Holographic](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/JIgimPdRB2L9BXi8.png)
**Concept:** Glowing holographic neon letters with AI-Garfield materializing
**Style:** Sci-fi meets nostalgia, ethereal glow
**Vibe:** Modern, mysterious, technological evolution

---

### **Version 4: "Vintage Postcard"**
![Vintage Postcard](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/Yr275Y9cRq7XFAW6.png)
**Concept:** 1970s travel postcard - "Greetings from Muncie, Indiana"
**Style:** Retro illustration, warm vintage colors
**Vibe:** Nostalgic invitation, heartland America

---

## **📊 SERIES OVERVIEW**

**Total Variations:** 10 unique interpretations

**Artistic Range:**
- ✅ **3 Minimalist/Graphic** - Clean, bold, iconic (Versions 1, 5, 8)
- ✅ **3 Atmospheric/Cinematic** - Beautiful lighting, journey (Versions 7, 9, 10)
- ✅ **2 Detailed/Narrative** - Story-rich, intricate (Versions 2, 6)
- ✅ **2 Stylized/Thematic** - Futuristic & nostalgic (Versions 3, 4)

**Wordplay Concept:**
Every poster plays on the classic "COMING SOON" movie trope but transforms it into "COMING HOME" - perfectly capturing the film's central theme while creating instantly recognizable, clever marketing.

**Marketing Applications:**
- **Teaser Campaign:** Versions 1, 3, 5, 7 (simple, intriguing)
- **Main Campaign:** Versions 2, 6, 10 (detailed, story-rich)
- **Specialty/Festival:** Versions 4, 8, 9 (artistic, nostalgic)
- **Social Media:** Version 1 (clean, shareable)
- **IMAX/Premium:** Versions 6, 7 (epic, cinematic)

---

## **🎯 DESIGN HIGHLIGHTS**

✨ **Consistent Branding** - "COMING HOME" as dominant element in all versions

✨ **Diverse Approaches** - From minimalist to epic montage

✨ **Accurate Characters** - Garfield per style guides, Jim Davis references used

✨ **Holographic AI-Garfield** - Translucent, glowing design in futuristic versions

✨ **Emotional Range** - Playful to profound, nostalgic to futuristic

✨ **Professional Quality** - Theatrical poster standards, ready for campaign

---

```sug
</suggestion>

</suggestion>
```

# 🏛️ **"COMING HOME" - BUILDING SILHOUETTE SERIES**
## *Deep Architectural Integration - 12 New Variations*

---

## **🎯 ARCHITECTURAL TRANSFORMATION**

### **Version 1: "Building AS Letters"**
![Building AS Letters](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/A1mrY4X0MYIHtdOB.png)
**Concept:** The building itself literally becomes the typography
**Integration:** Atrium = "O", roofline creates "M", architectural elements form letterforms
**Vibe:** Minimalist visual puzzle, sophisticated, golden hour elegance

---

### **Version 3: "Negative Space Building"**
![Negative Space Building](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/3GeNg7Q0F79gQtQQ.png)
**Concept:** Building silhouette cut out as negative space within bold letters
**Integration:** Sunset glows through building-shaped cutouts in typography
**Vibe:** Dramatic, graphic power, window to home

---

### **Version 10: "Building Shadow Typography"**
![Building Shadow Typography](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/LNOuNt0kyiFAsdVv.png)
**Concept:** Building's shadow spells "COMING HOME" on the ground
**Integration:** Aerial view, golden hour long shadows create natural letterforms
**Vibe:** Clever, subtle, sophisticated - light and architecture unite

---

## **🌅 REFLECTION & HORIZON**

### **Version 4: "Building Reflection Typography"**
![Building Reflection Typography](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/iwcWI5d8gI36tERz.png)
**Concept:** Building casts "COMING HOME" as reflection into twilight sky
**Integration:** Holographic AI-Garfield visible in atrium windows
**Vibe:** Magical realism, dreamy, building speaks to the heavens

---

### **Version 8: "Building Silhouette Horizon"**
![Building Silhouette Horizon](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/UwAKu4PFwoA1xEN9.png)
**Concept:** Building as horizon line, "COMING" above, "HOME" below
**Integration:** Perfect symmetry, Garfield walking toward building
**Vibe:** Zen minimalism, journey's end, peaceful arrival

---

## **🏗️ CONSTRUCTION & GROWTH**

### **Version 6: "Building Grows From Letters"**
![Building Grows From Letters](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/uMJzmq1XegHzMKSA.png)
**Concept:** Typography as foundation, building rising from the words
**Integration:** Letters literally support and birth the architecture
**Vibe:** Creation metaphor, vertical growth, from concept to reality

---

### **Version 11: "Deconstructed Building Letters"**
![Deconstructed Building Letters](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/Aof5J4Hk2R2v2vgp.png)
**Concept:** Letters made from exploded architectural elements
**Integration:** Glass panels, beams, bricks, window frames form letterforms
**Vibe:** Building's DNA, technical artistry, warm material textures

---

## **📐 TECHNICAL & BLUEPRINT**

### **Version 2: "Letters MADE OF Building"**
![Letters MADE OF Building](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/pEoqfRyL1K5GsZyA.png)
**Concept:** Each letter constructed from architectural floor plans
**Integration:** Blueprint lines and technical drawings form typography
**Vibe:** Architectural precision, sophisticated, design-forward

---

### **Version 9: "Architectural Section Cut"**
![Architectural Section Cut](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/PEKBNwrxIr5EJuhx.png)
**Concept:** Letters as section cuts revealing interior life
**Integration:** Cross-sections show atrium, rooms, people, activity inside
**Vibe:** Technical beauty, showing the heart and soul of the building

---

## **🌟 LIGHT & ATMOSPHERE**

### **Version 7: "Atrium Window Letters"**
![Atrium Window Letters](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/jVk1tv3liq18SkPH.png)
**Concept:** Lit windows form "COMING HOME" at night
**Integration:** Building facade becomes typographic canvas through light
**Vibe:** Community warmth, people visible as silhouettes, home glowing

---

### **Version 5: "Layered Transparency"**
![Layered Transparency](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/5ZDcMi0VeK24ddH5.png)
**Concept:** Transparent letters with building at different times of day
**Integration:** Multiple exposures showing dawn, noon, sunset, night through letters
**Vibe:** Time passage, complex artistry, building through the ages

---

### **Version 12: "Building Constellation"**
![Building Constellation](https://a.lovart.ai/artifacts/agent/NRrAJ9AdSqLULxFJ.png)
**Concept:** Stars form "COMING HOME" above warmly lit building
**Integration:** Building lights connect to constellation, earth to sky
**Vibe:** Magical, destiny, cosmic connection to home

---

## **📊 SERIES ANALYSIS**

**Total Variations:** 12 unique architectural integrations

**Integration Approaches:**
- ✅ **Direct Transformation** - Building becomes letters (Versions 1, 3, 10)
- ✅ **Reflection & Mirroring** - Building projects typography (Versions 4, 8)
- ✅ **Construction Metaphor** - Building grows from/made of letters (Versions 6, 11)
- ✅ **Technical/Blueprint** - Architectural drawings as typography (Versions 2, 9)
- ✅ **Light & Atmosphere** - Windows, shadows, stars form letters (Versions 5, 7, 12)

**Architectural Features Emphasized:**
- 🏛️ Three-story glass atrium (central in most designs)
- 🏛️ Distinctive roofline and building profile
- 🏛️ Window patterns and facade details
- 🏛️ Campus setting and landscape context
- 🏛️ Interior life and community visible

**Emotional Range:**
- **Minimalist Elegance:** Versions 1, 8, 10
- **Technical Sophistication:** Versions 2, 9, 11
- **Magical/Dreamy:** Versions 4, 5, 12
- **Community/Warmth:** Versions 6, 7
- **Dramatic Power:** Version 3

---

## **🎯 MARKETING APPLICATIONS**

**Teaser Campaign:** Versions 1, 3, 8, 10 (simple, intriguing silhouettes)

**Main Campaign:** Versions 4, 6, 7 (emotional, community-focused)

**Architectural/Design Press:** Versions 2, 9, 11 (technical, sophisticated)

**Festival/Awards:** Versions 5, 12 (artistic, elevated)

**Social Media:** Versions 1, 7, 10 (clean, shareable, striking)

**IMAX/Premium:** Versions 3, 4, 6 (dramatic, cinematic scope)

---

## **✨ DESIGN ACHIEVEMENTS**

🏆 **Deep Architectural Integration** - Building is not just present but fundamental to typography

🏆 **Paws Inc Recognition** - Distinctive atrium and roofline maintained throughout

🏆 **Diverse Approaches** - From minimalist to complex, technical to magical

🏆 **"Home" Emphasis** - Every design reinforces building as the heart of the story

🏆 **Professional Quality** - Theatrical poster standards, campaign-ready

🏆 **Clever Visual Solutions** - Each poster solves the building/typography puzzle uniquely

---

artifact.prompt_text:
CONCEPT 1: "THE SINGLE ORANGE PIXEL"
Ultra minimalist movie poster. Entire composition is cream/off-white (#FFF8E7) background. In the exact mathematical center of the poster, place one single tiny orange square pixel (#FF6600), approximately 8x8 pixels in size. This single orange pixel represents Garfield, home, the beginning, everything - ultimate Zen minimalism.

At the very bottom of the poster, in the smallest readable sans-serif font (approximately 8pt), centered: "GARFIELD: COMING HOME"

Below that, even smaller (6pt): "Written & Directed by Justin Massion  Executive Produced by Susannah Rosenthal"

This is controversial, bold, museum-worthy minimalism. Less-than-less-is-more. The viewer must work to find meaning. Rewards contemplation. Could hang in MoMA.

Style: Extreme minimalism, conceptual art, Zen philosophy, fine art poster design
Aspect ratio: 2:3 theatrical poster format
Color palette: Cream white background, single orange pixel, minimal black text

CONCEPT 2: "THE PENCIL LINE"
Saul Bass-inspired minimalist movie poster. Pure white background. One single continuous graphite pencil line (gray #4A4A4A) that flows from bottom to top, never breaking:

- Starts at bottom as outline of Garfield's sitting silhouette (recognizable cat ears, body shape)
- Transforms into a flowing path/road that curves upward through the composition
- Ends at top as simple outline of the Paws Inc building

The line IS the story - showing the entire journey in one unbroken stroke. Elegant, sophisticated, museum-worthy.

One tiny detail: where Garfield's nose would be, place a single small orange dot (#FF6600).

At bottom, minimal sans-serif text: "GARFIELD: COMING HOME"
Below: "Written & Directed by Justin Massion  Executive Produced by Susannah Rosenthal"

Style: Saul Bass minimalism, single line art, graphite drawing, conceptual design
Aspect ratio: 2:3 theatrical poster
Color: White background, gray pencil line, single orange dot

CONCEPT 5: "THE HORIZON LINE"
Terrence Malick-esque contemplative movie poster. About vastness, journey, longing, spiritual distance.

Composition: Entire poster divided by single perfect horizontal line (the horizon) positioned at exact middle of frame.

TOP HALF (sky): Gradient from cream (#FFF8E7) at horizon to pale orange (#FFD4A3) at top. Empty, vast, contemplative.

BOTTOM HALF (ground): Slightly darker cream/beige (#E8DCC8). Empty, minimal texture suggestion of earth/field.

On the horizon line: Microscopic silhouette of Paws Inc building (barely visible, tiny, must look for it).

In the lower third, approaching the horizon: Microscopic orange figure of Garfield walking toward building (tiny, distant, lonely).

The scale emphasizes vastness, distance, the long journey home. Spiritual, meditative, profound.

Bottom corner, minimal text: "GARFIELD: COMING HOME"
Credits: "Written & Directed by Justin Massion  Executive Produced by Susannah Rosenthal"

Style: Terrence Malick cinematography, contemplative minimalism, spiritual vastness
Aspect ratio: 2:3 theatrical poster
Color: Cream to pale orange gradient, minimal contrast

CONCEPT 3: "THE EMPTY CHAIR"
Edward Hopper-esque photorealistic movie poster. Interior scene at Paws Inc studio.

Main subject: Empty vintage wooden drawing chair/stool, positioned slightly off-center. The chair is waiting, implying absence. On the chair or nearby: a few pencils, maybe a coffee mug (cold, abandoned).

Lighting: Single beam of warm natural light coming from a window (not shown, just the light beam). Dust particles visible floating in the light shaft. The light falls across the empty chair, creating dramatic shadows.

Mood: Profound loneliness, anticipation, legacy, waiting for return. Photographic realism with emotional restraint.

Background: Soft focus studio environment, warm wood tones, cream walls, subtle.

NO Garfield visible. NO text except tiny credits at very bottom: "GARFIELD: COMING HOME" and below "Written & Directed by Justin Massion  Executive Produced by Susannah Rosenthal"

Style: Photorealistic, Edward Hopper loneliness, cinematic lighting, fine art photography
Aspect ratio: 2:3 theatrical poster
Color palette: Warm browns, cream, golden light, shadows

CONCEPT 4: "THE NEGATIVE SPACE CAT"
Japanese minimalist poster design. Clever visual puzzle using positive/negative space.

Main element: Simple geometric representation of Paws Inc building shown as clean architectural shape (warm orange #FF6600), positioned in upper-right portion of composition.

Key concept: The building casts a shadow/creates negative space that forms a PERFECT silhouette of Garfield sitting (recognizable cat ears, body, tail). The negative space cat should be obvious once seen but require a moment of observation.

Only TWO colors used:
- Building and positive elements: Warm orange (#FF6600)
- Background/shadow/negative space: Cream (#FFF8E7)

The shadow/negative space Garfield should be cream colored, formed by the orange building's shape and shadow.

Bottom text minimal: "GARFIELD: COMING HOME"
Credits: "Written & Directed by Justin Massion  Executive Produced by Susannah Rosenthal"

Style: Japanese minimalism, negative space mastery, geometric design, visual puzzle
Aspect ratio: 2:3 theatrical poster
Two-color design: Orange and cream only

CONCEPT 7: "THE WINDOW GRID"
Wes Anderson-esque symmetrical architectural poster. About finding home, the one light in darkness.

Composition: Perfect 3x3 grid of 9 identical windows from Paws Inc atrium building. Perfectly symmetrical, centered composition.

Windows: 8 windows are dark/empty (deep blue-black #1A1A2E), showing night/darkness.

Center window (middle of the 3x3 grid): Glowing with warm orange light (#FF6600), creating stark contrast. In this lit window, a tiny silhouette of Garfield is barely visible.

The grid should be architectural, geometric, precise. The building facade around the windows is minimal, dark, emphasizing the window grid pattern.

Mood: Loneliness meets hope. One light in the darkness. Finding home. Geometric beauty meets emotional narrative.

Bottom text: "GARFIELD: COMING HOME"
Credits: "Written & Directed by Justin Massion  Executive Produced by Susannah Rosenthal"

Style: Wes Anderson symmetry, architectural photography, geometric minimalism, high contrast
Aspect ratio: 2:3 theatrical poster
Color: Dark blue-black, single orange glowing window, minimal warm tones

CONCEPT 10: "THE SPLIT SCREEN"
Philosophical poster about evolution, time, technology, permanence through change.

Composition: Poster divided vertically down the exact middle into two halves.

LEFT HALF - "1978":
- Hand-drawn comic strip panel aesthetic
- Black ink on newsprint texture/cream paper
- Classic 2D Garfield sitting in iconic pose (hand-drawn, vintage comic style)
- Slight paper texture, authentic 1970s comic strip feel
- Label at top: "1978"

RIGHT HALF - "2026":
- Modern holographic/translucent Garfield in SAME POSE as left side
- Glowing, semi-transparent, futuristic rendering
- Neon orange and blue holographic effects
- Digital, technological, contemporary
- Label at top: "2026"

The two Garfields mirror each other - same pose, same expression, different eras. Shows consistency of character through technological evolution.

Bottom text centered: "GARFIELD: COMING HOME"
Credits: "Written & Directed by Justin Massion  Executive Produced by Susannah Rosenthal"

Style: Split composition, then/now comparison, analog vs digital, philosophical design
Aspect ratio: 2:3 theatrical poster

CONCEPT 9: "THE FOOTPRINTS"
Poetic minimalist poster. About traces, memory, the path home, journey through time.

Background: Cream/off-white (#FFF8E7), clean, minimal.

Main element: Series of small orange (#FF6600) cat paw prints starting at the bottom of the poster, walking upward toward the top. 

Key detail: The paw prints gradually get FAINTER as they move up the poster - starting bold and saturated at bottom, becoming increasingly transparent/faded as they approach the top. This implies:
- Journey through time
- Fading memory
- Distance traveled
- The passage of experience

At the top: Simple silhouette of Paws Inc building where the faintest paw prints lead.

The paw prints should be realistic cat paw shapes, walking naturally up the composition.

Bottom text: "GARFIELD: COMING HOME"
Credits: "Written & Directed by Justin Massion  Executive Produced by Susannah Rosenthal"

Style: Poetic minimalism, simple touching imagery, conceptual storytelling
Aspect ratio: 2:3 theatrical poster
Color: Cream background, orange paw prints (fading from solid to transparent)

CONCEPT 6: "THE TYPEWRITER KEY"
Intimate photorealistic macro photography poster. About creation, writing, storytelling, the tools of origin.

Main subject: Extreme close-up of single vintage typewriter key showing the letter "H" (for HOME). The key should be:
- Slightly worn, showing age and use
- Nostalgic patina, tactile detail visible
- Sepia/cream tones with orange accent on the key letter

The typewriter key fills most of the frame. You can see the metal arm/lever of the key extending down, slightly out of focus.

Background: Soft blur suggesting vintage typewriter mechanism, warm sepia tones, cream colors.

Mood: Nostalgic, intimate, tactile, about the physical act of creation. The tools that created Garfield.

Bottom text minimal: "GARFIELD: COMING HOME"
Credits: "Written & Directed by Justin Massion  Executive Produced by Susannah Rosenthal"

Style: Macro photography, shallow depth of field, nostalgic, tactile realism
Aspect ratio: 2:3 theatrical poster
Color palette: Sepia, cream, warm browns, orange accent on "H"

CONCEPT 8: "THE ORANGE CIRCLE"
Rothko-inspired color field poster with narrative depth. Meditative, abstract, profound.

Main element: Large perfect circle (Garfield orange #FF6600) centered on cream background (#FFF8E7). The circle should occupy approximately 60% of the poster height.

Within the orange circle: Subtle texture and imagery (barely visible, must look closely):
- Faint comic strip panels
